---
title: "Full run — results"
---

::: {.callout-note}
Numbers on this page are computed at render time from
`output/full/compare/summary.csv` and the coadd `.asdf` files, so a
re-rendered report picks up any re-run results.
:::

```{python}
#| echo: false
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from pathlib import Path

REPO = Path("../..").resolve()
df = pd.read_csv(REPO / "output/full/compare/summary.csv")
```

## Configuration

324 sims over 6 visits (2 passes × 3 dithers × segments 490 + 521),
320 skycells after `skycell_asn --product-type full`. The **design depth**
for this run is `pointings.design_depth = 6` (both passes × all 3 dithers);
the "design subset" below picks input sources whose (ra, dec) lands on a
coadd pixel of depth ≥ 6.

## Depth distribution

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Distribution of per-skycell depth metrics across the full run. Left: the maximum per-pixel depth reached in each skycell. Right: the median per-pixel depth (a proxy for how well each skycell is covered overall)."
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(10, 4))
for ax, col in zip(axes, ["max_depth", "median_depth"]):
    counts = df[col].value_counts().sort_index()
    ax.bar(counts.index, counts.values, edgecolor="k", lw=0.5)
    ax.set_xlabel(col)
    ax.set_ylabel("# skycells")
    ax.set_xticks(sorted(df[col].unique()))
    ax.grid(True, axis="y", alpha=0.3)
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Headline numbers

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "Headline recovery numbers, medians across all 320 skycells."
summary_tbl = pd.DataFrame({
    "metric": [
        "50%-completeness mag (all sources)",
        "50%-completeness mag (design subset)",
        "overall completeness (all sources)",
        "overall completeness (design subset)",
        "Kron flux residual (median, matched)",
        "astrometric residual (median, matched)",
    ],
    "median": [
        f"{df['mag_50pct_all'].median():.2f}",
        f"{df['mag_50pct_design'].median():.2f}",
        f"{df['completeness_all'].median():.3f}",
        f"{df['completeness_design'].median():.3f}",
        f"{df['median_dmag_all'].median():+.3f} mag",
        f"{df['median_dpos_all'].median():.3f}″",
    ],
})
summary_tbl
```

## How to read a per-skycell figure

Each per-skycell PNG has six panels. In brief:

- **Detection completeness** (top-left). Binned completeness vs input
  magnitude. The black curve is all in-footprint sources; the dashed red
  curve is the "design subset" (sources on pixels with depth ≥ 6). PSF
  and SER inputs are shown faintly for context. Dotted verticals mark
  the interpolated 50%-completeness magnitudes.
- **Flux residual** (top-middle). `recovered kron mag − input mag` vs
  input mag, matched sources only. Grey = sources below design depth,
  red = design-depth sources. The red dashed line is the design-subset
  median bias (typically +0.15–0.20 mag, a Kron-photometry property on
  extended sources).
- **PSF vs aper08** (top-right). `psf_mag − aper08_mag` vs `aper08_mag`,
  for recovered sources with `psf_flags = 0`, coloured by the *input*
  type: blue = input PSF, orange = input SER. Input PSFs should
  concentrate near Δ = 0 (PSF fit and aperture agree on point sources);
  input SERs should sit above Δ = 0 (PSF-fit underestimates extended
  flux). **Caveat: in these catalogs we see both clouds shifted up by
  ~+0.68 mag** — the PSF cloud sits near Δ ≈ +0.66 instead of 0, and
  the SER cloud near Δ ≈ +2.6 instead of ~+2. That shift matches the
  median CRDS gain (1.869 e⁻/DN → 2.5·log₁₀(1.869) = +0.68 mag), which
  strongly suggests `psf_flux` is emitted in DN/s-like units while
  `aper08_flux` and `kron_flux` are in nJy. We leave the panel uncorrected
  so the offset is visible; use `kron_flux` (which round-trips with
  `kron_abmag`) for any direct quantitative comparison rather than
  `psf_flux` until this is clarified upstream.
- **False-positive rate** (bottom-left). Fraction of recovered sources
  per recovered-mag bin that have no matching input within the match
  radius. Spurious detections / blends; usually well below 10% except at
  the faint end.
- **Astrometric residual** (bottom-middle). `|Δpos|` between matched
  recovered and input positions. Roman WFI is astrometrically very tight
  — typically ~0.015–0.020″, sub-coadd-pixel, across the full input-mag
  range.
- **Metadata panel** (bottom-right). Skycell identifiers, coadd shape,
  depth statistics, and headline numbers for both the "all" and
  "design" sub-populations.

## Per-skycell figure gallery

Top 60 skycells by `mean_depth` (deepest first). Click any thumbnail to
zoom.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
top = df.sort_values("mean_depth", ascending=False).head(60)
print("::: {layout-ncol=3}\n")
for _, row in top.iterrows():
    name = row["skycell"]
    cap = (f"{name} — "
           f"mean_depth={row['mean_depth']:.2f}, "
           f"mag50={row['mag_50pct_all']:.2f}")
    print(f"![{cap}](_plots/{name}.png)\n\n")
print(":::")
```

## Aggregated depth distribution

Pixel count per depth across every valid pixel of every skycell, with
the cumulative fraction at depth ≥ X overlaid. Read directly off the red
curve: the **fraction of pixels meeting a given depth** — e.g., at the
design-depth threshold of 6 for this run, ~21% of pixels qualify.

![](_depth_distribution.png){fig-alt="Per-pixel depth histogram with cumulative fraction overlay"}
